prompt: Our SaaS company provides B2B cloud-based HR software. We have 75 employees and €15M annual revenue. A customer just told us we need to be NIS2 compliant. Are we directly subject to NIS2, and if so what do we need to do to comply with Article 21?
assertions: Analyzes whether B2B SaaS qualifies as a 'cloud computing service' under Annex I (digital infrastructure) and concludes the company is likely directly in scope, since at 75 employees / €15M it meets the Art. 2(1) size threshold (medium-sized: 50+ employees or >€10M turnover)

Classifies the company as an IMPORTANT entity (medium-sized digital-infrastructure provider below the large-enterprise ceiling, and not one of the Art. 3(1) always-essential categories like TLD registries, DNS providers, or qualified trust service providers)

Walks through the Art. 21(2)(a)–(j) measures with concrete implementation guidance (risk policies, incident handling, business continuity/backup, supply chain, secure development/vulnerability handling, effectiveness assessment, hygiene and training, cryptography, HR/access control, MFA)
Mentions Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2690, which lays down the specific technical requirements (and significant-incident thresholds) binding on cloud computing service providers

Addresses jurisdiction/registration for digital providers: the main-establishment rule (Art. 26) and/or the entity registration requirement, plus the fact that supervision for important entities is ex-post (reactive)
